title: Triggers
description: Overview of trigger configuration in Streamer.bot
summary:
Introduction
The release of Streamer.bot 0.2.0 included a large overhaul to the events system, with the introduction of Triggers.

You now have more control over events and their execution through Trigger configuration options, and events can now trigger more than one action.

Triggers also introduced the ability to easily test your actions!

codeBlocks.label: Register Trigger
Execute Trigger
codeBlocks.code:
// Trigger the "Game Win" trigger using its name
  CPH.TriggerCodeEvent("gameResultWin", true);

codeBlocks.label: Register Nested Triggers
Execute Nested Triggers

description: Triggers custom code events outside of C#

parameters.description: Type the name of the custom code event to trigger

parameters.name: Use Args
parameters.description: Enable this option to enable parsing and replacement of `%variables%`{lang=cs} found within the Custom Event

title: Set Trigger State
description: Sets the Trigger State for an individual trigger on a specified action

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Select the desired Action to modify

parameters.name: Triggers
parameters.description: Select the Trigger from the selected Action

parameters.name: State
parameters.description:
Select the new state for the Trigger

- `Enabled`: Enable the selected Trigger
- `Disabled`: Disable the selected Trigger
- `Toggle`: Toggle the existing state of the selected Trigger

title: Send Remote Trigger
description: Send a trigger to another Streamer.bot instance
summary: React to the trigger on your other Streamer.bot instance with the Remote Instance Trigger

parameters.name: Instance
parameters.description:
Select the instance to send this trigger to

- `Any` will trigger *all* connected instances

::tip{to=/guide/integrations/streamerbot}
All instances must have the [Streamer.bot Website Integration](/guide/integrations/streamerbot) configured and enabled
::

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Name of the event to send.

- On the receiving instance, enter the same name in the [Remote Instance Trigger](/api/triggers/integrations/streamerbot-remote/remote-instance-trigger) configuration

parameters.name: Include Action's Arguments
parameters.description: Send all current action arguments with the trigger to the second instance

parameters.name: Arguments
parameters.description: Define any additional arguments to be sent to the second instance

title: Custom Code Event
description: Trigger for a Custom Code Event
summary:
Details
This event can **only** be triggered with the CPH.TriggerCodeEvent C# Method. You can use two methods. With the first method you can use the variables of the current action. With the second method you can specify a dictionary of variables for the trigger.

When registering a trigger your trigger will end up in the Custom folder under triggers. You can nest the trigger in multiple folders by using the catergories parameter. To give the trigger a name use the triggerName parameter. Use the eventName parameter for a name only used in C# for triggering the method.

Variables
Variables are dependent on the custom event.

title: Custom
summary:
Details
This event can **only** be triggered with the CPH.TriggerEvent C# Method.

Variables
Variables are dependent on the custom event.

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Input the event name of the custom event

- Leaving this blank is considered a catch-all and will trigger on **any** custom event

title: Coin Exchange
description: When a CrowdControl user exchanges for CrowdControl's Coin currency

parameters.name: Coin Type
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Free`
- `Paid`

parameters.name: Exchange Source
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Paypal`
- `Stripe`
- `Tiltify`
- `Twitch Bits`/`Twitch Channel Points`/`Twitch Sub`

parameters.name: Amount
parameters.description:
Filter events between a specified `Min` and `Max`

`Min` and `Max` are **inclusive** of the entered values

- `Any Value` - Leave both min and max empty to accept any value
- `Exact Value` - Enter only a `Max` to trigger on a specific value
- `Greater Than` - Enter only a `Min` to trigger on any value greater than the specified value
- `Range` - Enter a min and a max to trigger within a desired range

variables.name: coinType
variables.description: The type

variables.name: exchangeSource
variables.description: The source of the exchange

variables.name: anonymous
variables.description: If it was an anoymous exchange

variables.name: bonus
variables.description: Amount of bonus coins

title: Remote Instance Trigger
description: Triggers when a remote Streamer.bot instance trigger gets sent
summary:
This trigger is executed by the Send Remote Trigger sub-action, sent from another instance.

Additional variables will be populated for all arguments configured in the sending sub-action

parameters.name: Instance
parameters.description:
Select the instance to send this trigger to

- `Any` will trigger *all* connected instances

::tip{to=/guide/integrations/streamerbot}
All instances must have the [Streamer.bot Website Integration](/guide/integrations/streamerbot) configured and enabled
::

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Name of the event to trigger on

- Leave blank to trigger on **any** incoming events
- To trigger on a specific event, enter the same name configured in the [Send Remote Trigger](/api/triggers/integrations/streamerbot-remote/remote-instance-trigger) sub-action on another instance

variables.name: instanceId
variables.description: The instance ID

variables.name: instanceName
variables.description: Name of the instance

variables.name: instanceVersion
variables.description: Streamer.bot version of the instance

variables.name: instanceOs
variables.description: The operation system of the instance

title: First Words
description: Trigger for a Kick First Words
summary:
Details
This triggers when someone sends their first message of the stream.

The exact reset time is set in Platforms -> Kick -> First Words and is 12h by default. That means that Streamer.bot must have been closed for 12 hours in order to reset the First Words trigger.

If you want to reset the First Words trigger, you can do so by going to Platforms -> Kick -> First Words and clicking the Reset button. This will reset the First Words trigger and allow it to be triggered again.

variables.name: firstMessage
variables.description: indicator whether the user's message was the first message **ever** in the channel

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for the Kick present viewers event
summary:
Notes
The Kick present viewer acts more like a chat activity tracker. There is no "Live-Update" option for Kick as the API does not provide the viewer list.

Under the Platforms > Kick > Settings, you will find the options for the Present Viewers Trigger.

The slider behaves as a threshold. The timer runs every minute, and checks the current time minus the user's last active time, if this is less then the threshold, they are marked as present, otherwise they are marked as not present.  The trigger will be executed every minute.

title: Ad Mid-Roll
description: Trigger for a Twitch Ad Mid-Roll
summary:
**Removed in Streamer.bot v1.0.0**
Twitch has deprecated the PubSub API and all triggers relying on it have been removed. It is **not recommended** to rely on this trigger. - This trigger utilizes an **undocumented and unsupported** Twitch PubSub event which could be removed, without warning, in the future. This event triggers 5 seconds prior to the start of an ad.

title: Automatic Reward Redemption
description: Trigger for when default channel point rewards or Power-ups have been used
summary:
Details
The Automatic Reward Redemptions work for both **default rewards** as well as the newly introduced **Power-ups**.

Default Rewards
The default rewards are those made by Twitch like "Highlight My Message". You can either choose to enable or disable them, but you cannot delete and only restrictively edit them.

!Default Rewards

parameters.name: Reward
parameters.description: Select any or a specific automatic channel reward type.

variables.name: rewardType
variables.description:
Type of the reward

Possible values: `send_highlighted_message`, `random_sub_emote_unlock`, `chosen_sub_emote_unlock`, `chosen_modified_sub_emote_unlock`, `single_message_bypass_sub_mode`, `message_effect`, `gigantify_an_emote`, `celebration`

variables.name: rewardCost
variables.description: channel point cost of the reward

variables.name: unlockedEmoteId
variables.description: ID of the unlocked emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: unlockedEmoteName
variables.description: name of the unlocked emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: userInput
variables.description: the input message of the redeeming user (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: gigantifiedEmoteId
variables.description: the ID of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: gigantifiedEmoteName
variables.description: the name of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: gigantifiedEmoteUrl
variables.description: the image URL of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

title: Bot Whispers
description: Trigger for a Twitch Bot Whisper
summary:
Notes
The user sending the whisper must have a **verified phone number** (see the **Phone Number setting** in your **Security and Privacy settings**).

The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies, it will still indicate success even if the message is dropped.

Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of **40 unique recipients per day**. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

title: First Words
description: Trigger for a Twitch First Words
summary:
This triggers when someone sends their first message of the stream.

The exact reset time is set in Platforms -> Twitch -> Settings and is 12h by default. That means that StreamerBot must have been closed for 12 hours in order to reset the First Words trigger.

You can create a new action, then create a  Settings -> Reset First Words subaction and assign a Twitch -> Channel -> Stream Online trigger. This will automatically reset the First Words trigger once you go live without any time requirement.

parameters.name: User Name

parameters.name: Is UserId
parameters.description: Check this field if the text in the `User Name` field is a user id

variables.description: indicator whether the user's message was the first message **ever** in the channel

title: Whispers
summary:
Notes
The user sending the whisper must have a **verified phone number** (see the **Phone Number setting** in your **Security and Privacy settings**).

The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies, it will still indicate success even if the message is dropped.

Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of **40 unique recipients per day**. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for the Twitch present viewers event
summary:
Details
This triggers every 1-10 minutes, default every 5 minutes.

Notes
The present viewer tick will always happen, but you have the ability to have it "live update" from Twitch, or artificially mark someone as present.

Under the Platforms > Twitch > Settings, in the Present Viewers section, there are 2 settings, a Live Update check box, and a slider bar.

title: Present Viewers
summary:
Notes
The YouTube present viewer acts more like a chat activity tracker. There is no "Live-Update" option for YouTube as the API does not provide the viewer list.

Under the Platforms > YouTube > Settings, you will find the options for the Present Viewers Trigger.

The slider behaves as a threshold. The timer runs every minute, and checks the current time minus the user's last active time, if this is less then the threshold, they are marked as present, otherwise they are marked as not present.  The trigger will be executed every minute.

title: EventSource
name: EventSource
summary:
Within any action, the value of the eventSource variable can be retrieved using the CPH.GetSource() method, which returns an EventSource enum value.

This enum represents the source of the event that triggered the action.

Usage Example

title: Receiving Events
description: Learn how to subscribe to and handle events from the WebSocket server
summary:
Subscribing to Events

In order to receive events from the WebSocket server, you must first subscribe to them.

When using the **Streamer.bot Client** library and its .on(){lang} method, this is handled automatically!

codeBlocks.label: JSON Schema

title: WebSocket Events
description: Reference of all events emitted by the Streamer.bot WebSocket Server

title: Remote Triggers
description: Easily communicate between multiple Streamer.bot instances
summary:
You can use **Remote Triggers** to easily send data or execute actions on **multiple** Streamer.bot instances. It even works over remote networks!

To **send** events to another instance, we utilize the **Send Remote Trigger** sub-action

To **receive** events on another instance, we uitlize the **Remote Instance Trigger**

title: 24.trigger-update-0.2.3
description: Why are some triggers not firing as they should, after updating to 0.2.3 ?
summary:
BEFORE YOU STREAM
Be sure to check any triggers you have set up that have criteria (like Cheers, Twitch Subs, etc.), and may overlap in some way, and configure the Don't Run If settings for them. This can be done by right-clicking the trigger, then Don't Run If.
This is unfortunately not something that can be done with a configuration upgrade, as it has no idea what triggers are supposed to activate over others.

For a more detailed explanation about the Trigger update, read the Changelogs 0.2.3

title: BroadcastCustomMessage
name: BroadcastCustomMessage
summary:
Overview
A custom broadcast message, sent by the server, requested by one of the websocket clients.

Variables
Name | Description
----:|:------------
obsEvent.event | The OBS event in this case BroadcastCustomMessage
data | User-defined data
realm | Identifier provided by the sender
obsEvent.update-type | The update type of the OBS event in this case BroadcastCustomMessage
obsEvent._json | Everything above in a json format
{.vars-table}

---

name: Exiting
summary: Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ obsEvent.event | The OBS event in this case Exiting obsEvent.update-type | The update type of the OBS event in this case Exiting obsEvent._json | Everything above in a json format --- - **OBS Studio Events Archive *Go Back*** - **OBS Websocket documentation *This links to the GitHub documentation of this specific event*** {.btn-grid my-5}

title: Media
name: Media
summary:
Added in obs-websocket v4.9.0

**Note**: These events are only emitted when something actively controls the media/VLC source. In other words, the source will never emit this on its own naturally.
* MediaPlaying
* MediaPaused
* MediaRestarted
* MediaStopped
* MediaNext
* MediaPrevious
* MediaStarted
* MediaEnded

title: Recording
name: Recording
summary:
Events related to current recording status
* **RecordingStarting *A request to start recording has been issued***
* **RecordingStarted *Recording started successfully***
* **RecordingStopping *A request to stop recording has been issued***
* **RecordingStopped *Recording stopped successfully***
* **RecordingPaused *Current recording paused***
* **RecordingResumed *Current recording resumed***
{.btn-grid .my-5}

title: SceneItemTransformChanged
name: SceneItemTransformChanged
summary:
Variables

obsEvent.event  The OBS event in this case SceneItemTransformChanged
obsEvent.item-id  Scene item ID
obsEvent.item-name  Name of the item in the scene
obsEvent.scene-name  Name of the scene
obsEvent.transform.bounds.alignment  Alignment of the bounding box
obsEvent.transform.bounds.type  Type of bounding box, Can be OBS_BOUNDS_STRETCH, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_INNER, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_OUTER, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_TO_WIDTH, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_TO_HEIGHT, OBS_BOUNDS_MAX_ONLY or OBS_BOUNDS_NONE.
obsEvent.transform.bounds.x  Width of the bounding box
obsEvent.transform.bounds.y  Height of the bounding box
obsEvent.transform.crop.bottom  The number of pixels cropped off the bottom of the source before scaling
obsEvent.transform.crop.left  The number of pixels cropped off the left of the source before scaling
obsEvent.transform.crop.right  The number of pixels cropped off the right of the source before scaling
obsEvent.transform.crop.top  The number of pixels cropped off the top of the source before scaling
obsEvent.transform.height  Scene item height (base source height multiplied by the vertical scaling factor)
obsEvent.transform.locked  If the source's transform is locked
obsEvent.transform.position.alignment  The point on the source that the item is manipulated from. The sum of 1=Left or 2=Right, and 4=Top or 8=Bottom, or omit to centre on that axis
obsEvent.transform.position.x  The x position of the source from the left
obsEvent.transform.position.y  The y position of the source from the top
obsEvent.transform.rotation  The clockwise rotation of the item in degrees around the point of alignment
obsEvent.transform.scale.filter  The scale filter of the source. Can be OBS_SCALE_DISABLE, OBS_SCALE_POINT, OBS_SCALE_BICUBIC, OBS_SCALE_BILINEAR, OBS_SCALE_LANCZOS or OBS_SCALE_AREA
obsEvent.transform.scale.x  The x-scale factor of the source
obsEvent.transform.scale.y  The y-scale factor of the source
obsEvent.transform.sourceHeight  Base source (without scaling) of the source
obsEvent.transform.sourceWidth  Base width (without scaling) of the source
obsEvent.transform.visible  If the source is visible
obsEvent.transform.width  Scene item width (base source width multiplied by the horizontal scaling factor)
obsEvent.update-type  The update type of the OBS event in this case SceneItemTransformChanged
obsEvent._json  Everything above in a json format

---

title: SwitchScenes
name: SwitchScenes
summary:
Variables
Name | Description
----:|:------------
obsEvent.event | The OBS event in this case SwitchScenes
obsEvent.scene-name | The name of the scene it switches to
obsEvent.sources[#] | The settings of the sources in the scene you switched to
obsEvent.update-type | The update type of the OBS event in this case SwitchScenes
obsEvent._json | Everything above in a json format

Example
When this event is linked to an action you can add this for example:

This will disable channel points on your "brb" scene and enable it on your "game" scene.

codeBlocks.code:
if ("obsEvent.scene-name" Equals "brb") do "disable channel points action" then "break"
if ("obsEvent.scene-name" Equals "game") do "enable channel points action" then "break"

title: Sources
name: Sources
summary:
Events related to source & filter changes
* **SourceCreated *A source has been created***
* **SourceDestroyed *A source has been removed***
* **SourceVolumeChanged *The volume of a source has changed.***

* **SourceAudioSyncOffsetChanged *The audio sync offset of a source has changed***

* **SourceRenamed *A source has been renamed***
* **SourceFilterAdded *A filter was added to a source***
* **SourceFilterRemoved *A filter was removed from a source***

title: OBS Studio Events (Archive)

description: Reference of all configurable events from OBS Studio (obs-websocket-4.9.1)

title: CurrentProfileChanged
name: CurrentProfileChanged
summary: Overview The current profile has changed. Variables Name ----: obsEvent.profileName Name of the new profile Data Fields :--- *1*{.obs-version-badge} *v5.0.0*{.obs-version-badge}

title: CurrentProfileChanging
name: CurrentProfileChanging
summary: Overview The current profile has begun changing. Variables Name ----: obsEvent.profileName Name of the current profile Data Fields :--- *1*{.obs-version-badge} *v5.0.0*{.obs-version-badge}

title: CurrentSceneCollectionChanged
name: CurrentSceneCollectionChanged
summary:
Overview
The current scene collection has changed.

Note: If polling has been paused during CurrentSceneCollectionChanging, this is the cue to restart polling.

Variables
Name  
----:
obsEvent.sceneCollectionName  Name of the new scene collection

title: CurrentSceneCollectionChanging
name: CurrentSceneCollectionChanging
summary:
Overview
The current scene collection has begun changing.

Note: We recommend using this event to trigger a pause of all polling requests, as performing any requests during a scene collection change is considered undefined behavior and can cause crashes!

Variables
Name  
----:
obsEvent.sceneCollectionName  Name of the current scene collection

title: VendorEvent
name: VendorEvent
summary:
Overview
An event has been emitted from a vendor.

A vendor is a unique name registered by a third-party plugin or script, which allows for custom requests and events to be added to obs-websocket. If a plugin or script implements vendor requests or events, documentation is expected to be provided with them.

Variables
Name  
----:
obsEvent.vendorName  Name of the vendor emitting the event
obsEvent.eventType  Vendor-provided event typedef
obsEvent.eventData  Vendor-provided event data. {} if event does not provide any data

title: OBS Studio Events Guide
name: OBS Studio Events Guide
summary:
Overview
OBS Studio events let you trigger actions on things that change in OBS, some of these events even have variables so you can see what's changed.

Configuration
!overview
!add obs event currentprogramscenechanged

To use an event you need to go to the Stream Apps --> OBS tab and add and an event (<kbd>right-click</kbd> at the bottom of this page, where it says Event). Now fill out the data so choose an event out of the list for this example CurrentProgramSceneChanged and choose an action, now with this example when you change scenes the selected action will trigger and it will give these variables this can be used for a ton of things e.g. for an if statement as shown on the page or for a send message to channel sub-action with: %obsEvent.sceneName%. But it can be used for more like for the StreamStateChanged event, this most oftenly used with if statements as shown on the page, simply just add them in the selected action and it all works.

title: RecordStateChanged
name: RecordStateChanged
summary:
Overview
The state of the record output has changed.

Variables
Name  
----:
obsEvent.outputActive  Whether the output is active
obsEvent.outputState  The specific state of the output
obsEvent.outputPath  File name for the saved recording, if record stopped. null otherwise

obsEvent.outputState
Name | Description
----:|:------------
OBS_WEBSOCKET_OUTPUT_STOPPING | You have send the request to stop recording
OBS_WEBSOCKET_OUTPUT_STOPPED | You have successfully stopped recording
OBS_WEBSOCKET_OUTPUT_STARTING | You have send the request to start recording
OBS_WEBSOCKET_OUTPUT_STARTED | You have successfully started recording

codeBlocks.code:
if ("obsEvent.outputState" Equals "OBS_WEBSOCKET_OUTPUT_STARTED") do "<start recording action>" then "break"
if ("obsEvent.outputState" Equals "OBS_WEBSOCKET_OUTPUT_STOPPED") do "<stop recording action>" then "break"

title: Events
name: Events
description: Reference of all events that can be used to trigger actions in Streamer.bot
summary:
• Triggers
    Reference of all available triggers and their populated variables.
All event sources below can be used to trigger Actions within Streamer.bot On each page you will find associated events along with their respective variables.

title: Ad Midroll
name: Ad Midroll
summary:
Overview
This event gets triggered 5 seconds before the ad starts, you cannot change this duration. If you want an event for when the ad starts, use the Ad Run event

Variables
Name | Description
----:|:------------
ad.commercialId | The ID of the ad that's about to run
ad.jitterTime | How long until the ad runs (ms)
ad.warmupTime | How long until the ad runs (ms)
{.vars-table}

---

title: Ad Run
name: Ad Run
summary:
Overview This event gets triggered when the ad starts. If you're looking for an event that triggers 5 seconds before the ad, then use the Ad Midroll event Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ adLength | The length of the ad in seconds adScheduled | If this ad was a scheduled ad
True/False {.vars-table} ---

title: Announcement
name: Announcement
summary:
Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ announceColor | The color of the announcement
DEFAULT, BLUE, RED, ORANGE, PURPLE message | The announcement message messageStripped | The announcement message without emotes emoteCount | The number of emotes in the message emotes | The emotes in the message, this is a List<> object badgeCount | The number of badges for the user making the announcement badges | The badges for the user making the announcement
This is a List<> object {.vars-table} --- - **Twitch Events *Go Back*** {.btn-grid .my-5}

title: Charity
name: Charity
summary:
Overview
Charity Started, Completed, Progress and Donation events.

!twitch-charity-overview.png

Variables
> Twitch is providing amount values for Charity calls as whole numbers, so $42.00 will return as 4200.
{.is-warning}

title: Chat Message
name: Chat Message
summary:
Event Information :----|:------------: Twitch Service: | Chat Client Added in: | *v0.0.50*{.version-badge} Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ msgId | Twitch's message ID role | What role the user has (1-4)
4=Broadcaster 3=Mod 2=VIP 1=Viewer isSubscribed | Boolean value indicating the user's subscription status
True/False color | Hex value of user's chat color
a random value will be selected if the user has not set one colorR | Hex value for Red component of the color variable colorG | Hex value for Green component of the color variable colorB | Hex value for Blue component of the color variable message | Message that was sent to chat emoteCount | How many Twitch emotes were found emotes | Comma Separated list of Twitch emotes found messageStripped | The chat message with emotes stripped messageCheermotesStripped | The chat message with cheer emotes stripped isHighlight | Boolean for message highlight property
True/False bits | Number of bits the message has isAction | Boolean value indicating the message is a /me action
True/False isReply| Boolean value indicating the message is a reply to another message
True/False replyTo| The username the message is replying to
Only populated is isReply is True firstMessage | Boolean value indicating the message is from a first time chatter in the channel
True/False *v0.1.8*{.version-badge} {.vars-table} ---

title: Cheers
name: Cheers
summary:
Overview
In this tab you can assign actions to your twitch cheer events so every time you get bits cheered on your twitch channel, the action will run but here is the fun part you can have variations for a specified ranges.

So to do this open your Streamer.bot and navigate to the Cheers tab. You will find this in Settings the click Events now click the Cheers it should now look like the screenshot displayed below.

!twitch_cheers.png

title: Community Goal
name: Community Goal
summary:
Overview
In this tab you can assign actions to your Community Goal that you have set up through your Twitch dashboard.

To get to this tab you need to click through the following tabs, First click the Settings tab next click on the Events tab then finally click the Community Goal tab. You should now see a window just like the image below.

!communitygoal1.png

title: First Words
name: First Words
summary:
Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ msgId | Twitch's message ID role | What role the user has (1-4)
4=Broadcaster 3=Mod 2=VIP 1=Viewer isSubscribed | Boolean value indicating the user's subscription status
True/False color | Hex value of user's chat color
a random value will be selected if the user has not set one colorR | Hex value for Red component of the color variable colorG | Hex value for Green component of the color variable colorB | Hex value for Blue component of the color variable message | Message that was sent to chat emoteCount | How many Twitch emotes were found emotes | Comma Separated list of Twitch emotes found messageStripped | The chat message with emotes stripped messageCheermotesStripped | The chat message with cheer emotes stripped isHighlight | Boolean for message highlight property
True/False bits | Number of bits the message has isAction | Boolean value indicating the message is a /me action
True/False isReply| Boolean value indicating the message is a reply to another message
True/False replyTo| The username the message is replying to
Only populated is isReply is True firstMessage | Boolean value indicating the message is from a first time chatter in the channel
True/False *v0.1.8*{.version-badge} {.vars-table} --- - **Twitch Events *Go Back*** {.btn-grid .my-5}

title: Follows Event
name: Follows Event
summary:
Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ user | The user that followed the broadcaster userName | The login name of the user that followed the broadcaster userId | Unique user identifier userType | pecifies which streaming service the triggering user is coming from *v0.1.8+*{.version-badge}
twitch or youtube isSubscribed | Boolean value indicating the sender's subscription status
True/False isModerator | Boolean value indicating the sender's moderator status
True/False isVip | Boolean value indicating the sender's VIP status
True/False eventSource | specifies which streaming service the triggering event is coming from *v0.1.8+*{.version-badge}
twitch or youtube {.vars-table} > This will also include all the Broadcaster Variables {.is-success} ---

title: General
name: General
summary:
The General tab covers 10 Simple events and a single action can be assigned to each;

Events

Name | Description
----:|:------------
Follows | When someone follows your channel
Whispers  If the whisper itself contains a command, the command action will trigger instead of this generic one
Present Viewers  Populates a dictionary of special variables for each user in chat
Chat Message  If the message contains a command, the command action will trigger instead of this generic one
Announcement | When an announcement is made is chat
First Words  This runs alongside the Chat Message event so if you have actions set for both, they will both run
Message Deleted  When this event is triggered, the message argument will be populated with the message being deleted
User Timed Out  This event will populate the duration argument with the time-out length
User Banned  This action will trigger on any user being banned, however if the user has never been present in chat while Streamer.bot is running there will be no username data
Ad Run | When a commercial is triggered on your channel by any source
{.vars-table}

title: Gift Bombs
name: Gift Bombs
summary:
> If you want to know who those subs are gifted to, you need to use the Gift Sub event with the variable recipientUser
{.is-info}

Overview
A Gift bomb event is the most typical event for subscription gifting and is triggered when a gift sub is purchased but no specific user is targeted, i.e. the recipient is random

A gift bomb happens when there are 2+ gift subs.  In Twitch Chat you'll see the message **so and so is gifting # subs to the community** followed by a slew of Gift Subs

title: Gift Subscription
name: Gift Subscription
summary:
Overview
A Gift Sub event is triggered when someone buys a subscription for a named person other than themselves.

!events-gift-sub.png

You can configure events to trigger on specific tiers or set to Generic to trigger on any tier
Different alerts can also be configured for anonymous and non-anonymous gifts or you can set to Either to trigger on both

title: Hosts
name: Hosts
summary:
Overview
Streamer.bot monitor for both a user starting a host of your channel. The actions for this can be configured on the Host tab

Generic
The Generic action is run on any incoming host that does not match a defined range

Viewer Ranges
If you would like a special alert to override the generic action for incoming hosts of specific sizes you can define the ranges and the Action to assign to them here.
> 
> You can add as many ranges as you like but there is no way to define an unlimited upper bound, so if you are using ranges make sure your largest one has a Max value that will be big enough for your needs, otherwise Streamer.bot will default back to the Generic action
{.is-info}

title: Hype Train
name: Hype Train
summary:
Overview
In this tab you can assign actions to the various levels of the hype train.

To get to this tab you need to click through the following tabs, First click the Settings tab next click on the Events tab then finally click the Hype Train tab. You should now see a window just like the image below.

!hypetrain.png

title: Message Deleted Event
name: Message Deleted Event
summary: Variables Name | Description ----:|:------------ message | The message that was deleted from chat {.vars-table} --- - **Twitch Events *Go Back*** {.btn-grid .my-5}

title: Present Viewers Event
name: Present Viewers Event
summary:
Overview
The present viewer tick will always happen, but you have the ability to have it "live update" from twitch, or artificially mark someone as present.

Under the Present Viewer action selector, there are 2 setttings, a Live Update check box, and a slider bar.

When Live Update is checked, the slider next to it is how often this update will occur, between 1 and 10 minutes, this will also still execute the action at this interval.

title: Raids
name: Raids
summary:
Overview
Streamer.bot can handle alerts for both an incoming and outgoing raid from the channel, the actions for both can be configured on the Raid tab

!raid.png

Incoming Raids

title: Shoutout Created
name: Shoutout Created
summary:
Variables
The user who was given the shoutout will be contained in the target variables.

Name | Description
----:|:------------
shoutoutId | Twitch's internal ID for the shoutout
targetUserId | The user's id
targetUserLogin | The user's login name
targetUserDisplayName | The user's display name
targetUserPrimaryColorHex | The user's primary color in hex
targetUserProfileImageURL | The user's profile image
{.vars-table}

---

name: Shoutout Received
summary:

This event runs when an other streamer sends you a shoutout.

Variables
Name | Description

viewerCount | The viewer count from the sending party
{.vars-table}

> This event will also include the Broadcaster and the User variables.
{.is-success}

----:|:------------
endedAt | The date time when the stream went offline

title: Stream Update
name: Stream Update
summary:
Overview The Stream Update event is triggered any time your stream category or title changes. Variables The following variables are available from this event: Name | Description ----:|:------------ status | Current stream title gameUpdate | Boolean value denoting whether the game category has changed
True / False statusUpdate | Boolean value denoting whether the stream title has changed
True / False gameId | Stream's current game category ID gameName | Stream's current game name oldStatus | Previous stream status oldGameId | Previous game category ID oldGameName | Previous game name gameBoxArt | URL for current game boxart image *v0.1.5+*{.version-badge} oldGameBoxArt | URL for previous game boxart image *v0.1.5+*{.version-badge} {.vars-table}

title: Subscription
name: Subscription
summary:
Overview

Sub
This event triggers when a user first subscribes themselves to the channel and has not recieved any gift subs.

Actions can be assigned to Generic Action which will trigger on every first subscription 
You can also assign actions to specific Tiers Twitch Prime Tier 1 Tier 2 Tier3

title: Twitch Events
name: Twitch Events
description: Reference of all available Twitch events
summary:
Streamer.bot constantly listens for events from your configured Twitch account and can perform actions for each of them.

> Some Twitch events may require Affiliate status and some may be unavailable if prohibited by local laws.
{.is-info}

Standard Variables
User
User variables are added when a specific person invokes it, this is with chat messages, cheers, whispers, etc. when something like a Ad Run, Present Viewers event triggers it won't generate variables because these aren't user specific.

title: WebSocket Server Events (PRIVATE)
name: WebSocket Server Events (PRIVATE)
description: Documentation of events that are emitted by the Streamer.bot WebSocket Server
summary:
Event Output Format
Tab {.tabset}
Schema

Example

twitch
* **AdMidRoll**
* **AdRun**
* **Announcement**
* **BotWhisper**
* **BroadcastUpdate** *?*{.version-badge}
* **CharityCompleted**
* **CharityDonation**
* **CharityProgress**
* **CharityStarted**
* **ChatCleared**
* **ChatMessage**
* **ChatMessageDeleted**
* **Cheer**
* **CoinCheer**
* **CommunityGoalContribution**
* **CommunityGoalEnded**
* **FirstWord**
* **Follow**
* **GiftBomb**
* **GiftSub**
* **GoalBegin**
* **GoalEnd**
* **GoalProgress**
* **HypeTrainEnd**
* **HypeTrainLevelUp**
* **HypeTrainStart**
* **HypeTrainUpdate**
* **PollCompleted**
* **PollCreated**
* **PollUpdated**
* **PredictionCanceled**
* **PredictionCompleted**
* **PredictionCreated**
* **PredictionLocked**
* **PredictionUpdated**
* **PresentViewers** *BROKEN*{.version-badge}
* **PyramidBroken**
* **PyramidSuccess**
* **Raid**
* **ReSub**
* **RewardCreated**
* **RewardDeleted**
* **RewardRedemption**
* **RewardUpdated**
* **ShieldModeBegin**
* **ShieldModeEnd**
* **ShoutoutCreated**
* **ShoutoutReceived**
* **StreamOffline**
* **StreamOnline** *BROKEN*{.version-badge}{.disabled}
* **StreamUpdate**
* **StreamUpdateGameOnConnect**
* **Sub**
* **SubCounterRollover**
* **UserBanned**
* **UserTimedOut**
* **UserUntimedOut**
* **Whisper**
{.btn-grid .my-5}

title: Trigger Alert
name: Trigger Alert
summary:
Overview
Limited PolyPop support is now available in Streamer.bot *v0.1.8+*{.version-badge}

This sub-action connects to PolyPop via WebSocket to trigger alerts.

!polypop-streamerbot-notconnected.png

title: Changed
name: Changed
summary:
Overview
This triggers when a file gets created in the folder you're watching.

For a detailed guide on how the File/Folder Watcher works in Streamer.bot see this page.

Configuration
Watcher
Select any or a specific watcher from the Settings --> File/Folder Watcher tab

title: Created
name: Created
summary:
Overview
This triggers when a file gets created in the folder you're watching.

For a detailed guide on how the File/Folder Watcher works in Streamer.bot see this page.

Configuration
Watcher
Select any or a specific watcher from the Settings --> File/Folder Watcher tab

title: Deleted
name: Deleted
summary:
Overview
This triggers when a file gets created in the folder you're watching.

For a detailed guide on how the File/Folder Watcher works in Streamer.bot see this page.

Configuration
Watcher
Select any or a specific watcher from the Settings --> File/Folder Watcher tab

title: Renamed
name: Renamed
summary:
Overview
This triggers when a file gets renamed in the folder you're watching.

For a detailed guide on how the File/Folder Watcher works in Streamer.bot see this page.

Configuration
Watcher
Select any or a specific watcher from the Settings --> File/Folder Watcher tab

title: File/Folder Watcher
name: File/Folder Watcher
summary: - **Changed *When a file is changed*** - **Created *When a file is created, works great with the folder watcher*** - **Deleted *When a file is deleted, works great with the folder watcher*** - **Renamed *When a file is renamed, works great with the folder watcher*** {.btn-grid .my-5} --- - **Triggers Reference *Go Back*** {.btn-grid .my-5}

title: HotKey Press
name: HotKey Press
summary:
Overview
The HotKey Press trigger is used for when you press a key on one of your keyboards.

For a detailed guide on how Hot Keys work in Streamer.bot see this page.

Configuration
HotKey
Select a hot key that is defined in the Hot Keys tab.

title: Hot Keys
name: Hot Keys

title: Processes
name: Processes

title: Added
name: Added
summary:
Overview
The Quote Added trigger gets triggered when you add a quote.

For a detailed guide on how Quotes work in Streamer.bot see this page.

Variables
Name | Description
----:|:------------
quote | The quote content
quoteID | The numeric quote id
quoteUser | The user that added the quote,
quoteGame | The game the broadcaster was playing at the time
quoteTime | The date it was logged
{.vars-table}

title: Show
name: Show
summary:
Overview
The Quote Show trigger gets triggered when you show a quote.

For a detailed guide on how Quotes work in Streamer.bot see this page.

Variables
Name | Description
----:|:------------
quote | The quote content
quoteID | The numeric quote id
quoteUser | The user that added the quote,
quoteGame | The game the broadcaster was playing at the time
quoteTime | The date it was logged
{.vars-table}

title: Quote
name: Quote

title: Timed Actions
name: Timed Actions
summary:
Overview
The timed actions trigger runs when any or a selected timer reaches its interval.

Go to the Timed Actions page for a detailed explanation on how Timed Actions work.

Configuration
Timers
Select a timer from the Settings --> Timed Actions tab

name: Voice Control

The Websocket Client Close Trigger is used for when any or a selected client closes.

Client

John Doe attributes.is_follower | If the user is a follower.
True/False attributes.last_charge_date | The date the pledge was created.
2022-07-27T15:11:03.000+00:00 attributes.last_charge_status | The charge status of the pledge.
True/False attributes.lifetime_support_cents | The amount of cents the user has given (in U.S. cents). attributes.next_charge_date | Then next charge date of the user. attributes.note | The note that the user has given. attributes.patron_status | If the user is subscribed to your patreon. attributes.pledge_cadence | Number of months between charges. attributes.pledge_relationship_start | The date that the user subsribed to your patron. attributes.will_pay_amount_cents | The amount of cents the user has given (in U.S. cents). user.Attributes.about | The about text of the user. user.Attributes.created | When the account of the user was created. user.Attributes.full_name | The full name of the user.

John user.Attributes.last_name | The last name of the user.
Doe user.Attributes.hide_pledges | If the user has it's pledges hidden. user.Attributes.image_url | The profile image of the patron. user.Attributes.is_creator | If the user is a creator.

shopify.subtotal_price | The subtotal price of the order e.g. 244.98

shopify.confirmed | The confirmed status True/False
shopify.total_discounts | The total amount of discount e.g. 5.00
shopify.total_line_items_price | The total price e.g. 249.98
shopify.buyer_accepts_marketing | If the customer accepts marketing True/False

shopify.referring_site | The reffering site
shopify.landing_site | The landing site
shopify.total_price_usd | The total price in USD
shopify.order_number | The order number

title: Events
name: Events
description: Reference of all events that are used in TwitchSpeaker
summary: Twitch - **Follow *When an user follows*** - **Sub *When an user subscribes*** - **SubWithMessage *When an user subscribes with a message*** - **Resub *When an user re-subscribes*** - **ResubWithMessage *When an user re-subscribes with a message*** - **GiftSub *When an user gifts a sub*** - **GiftSubAnon *When an anonymous user gifts a sub*** - **Raid *When you receive a raid*** - **Cheer *When you receive a cheer*** - **CheerAnon *When you receive an anonymous cheer*** - **ChannelPoints *When an user redeems a channel point*** - **GiftBomb *When an user throws a giftbomb*** - **GiftBombWithTotal *When an user throws a giftbomb including total variable*** - **HypeTrainStarted *When a hype train starts*** - **HypeTrainLevelUp *When a hype train levels up*** - **HypeTrainEnded *When a hype train ends*** - **GoalContribution *When an user contributes to a goal*** - **GoalFinsihed *When a hype train finishes*** {.btn-grid .my-5} StreamElements - **StreamElementsTip *When an user donates via StreamElements*** {.btn-grid .my-5} StreamLabs - **Donation *When an user donates via StreamLabs*** - **Merchandise *When an user buys merchandise*** {.btn-grid .my-5}

title: Events
name: Events
summary:
In the TwitchSpeaker Events Tab you will find a series of configurable event for the TTS

So, to get to this tab all you need to do is to open TwitchSpeaker then click the Events tab you will land on this page shown below.

!events-tab-overview.png
